prompt: Critique this design idea for our Sponsored Ads platform: we want to add a 'Duplicate Campaign' button to each row in the campaign table. Clicking it copies all settings from that campaign and opens a pre-filled campaign creation form in a side drawer. The form highlights what was copied, lets the user rename it, and has a 'Save as Draft' vs 'Launch Now' option at the bottom. Should we build this exactly as described, or is there a better way?

assertions.name: produces_rigor_matrix_table
assertions.description: Output contains a markdown table with all four dimensions: Feasibility, UX, Business, Delight
assertions.check: file contains '| Feasibility' AND '| UX' AND '| Business' AND '| Delight'

assertions.name: includes_numeric_scores
assertions.description: Each dimension row has a numeric score (1-5)
assertions.check: file contains score values in the matrix rows (digits 1-5 in table cells)

assertions.name: has_verdict_section
assertions.description: Output includes a Verdict section that is actionable — says what to build, defer, or cut

assertions.name: covers_all_four_lenses
assertions.description: Each of the four agent lenses produces a distinct analysis section
assertions.check: file contains 'Feasibility Analysis' AND 'UX Analysis' AND 'Business Analysis' AND 'Delight Analysis'

assertions.name: surfaces_conflicts_or_agreements
assertions.description: Synthesis section identifies where agents agree or conflict
assertions.check: file contains 'Conflicts' or 'conflicts' or 'agrees' or 'alignment' in synthesis section

assertions.name: opinionated_not_hedged
assertions.description: Verdict uses direct language, not wishy-washy hedging ('this will' not 'this might')
assertions.check: qualitative — reviewer checks that verdict takes a clear position
